Data.Description: A tool that enables creating and managing UiPath RPA projects via Studio IPC.

Options.Description: Project directory to match against running Studio instances

Subcommands.Name: instances [options]
Subcommands.Description: Discover running Studio instances

Subcommands.Name: studio [options]
Subcommands.Description: Control the Studio process

Subcommands.Name: restore [options] <project-dir> [output-path]
Subcommands.Description: Restore NuGet dependencies for a UiPath project. With outputPath, additionally assembles dependencies in that directory for CI/CD and air-gapped workflows where dependencies must be shipped across an isolation boundary. When provided, outputPath must be OUTSIDE the project directory tree.

Subcommands.Name: analyze [options] <project-dir>
Subcommands.Description: Validate and analyze a UiPath project

Subcommands.Name: build [options] <project-dir>
Subcommands.Description: Build (compile) a UiPath project

Subcommands.Name: pack [options] <project-dir> <output-path>
Subcommands.Description: Pack a UiPath project into a .nupkg (compiles, validates, and bundles).

Subcommands.Name: uia [options]
Subcommands.Description: UI Automation CLI commands

Subcommands.Name: test-data [options]
Subcommands.Description: Manage data sources for test cases

Subcommands.Name: init [options]
Subcommands.Description: Initialize a new UiPath project from a template

Subcommands.Name: templates [options]
Subcommands.Description: Discover project templates

Subcommands.Name: files [options]
Subcommands.Description: Operate on individual files

Subcommands.Name: analyzer-rules [options]
Subcommands.Description: Inspect the project's workflow analyzer rules

Subcommands.Name: validate [options]
Subcommands.Description: Validate a single file or the entire project and return diagnostics, filtered by severity. If filePath is omitted, scopes to the whole project. By default, runs validation before returning diagnostics; set skipValidation to true to read the cached results from the last validation. Use minSeverity 'error' for quick checks; 'warning' (default) or 'info' for thorough reviews.

Subcommands.Name: packages [options]
Subcommands.Description: Manage NuGet packages used by the project

Subcommands.Name: data-fabric-entities [options]
Subcommands.Description: Manage Data Fabric entity bindings

Subcommands.Name: activities [options]
Subcommands.Description: Discover and inspect activities

Subcommands.Name: workflow-examples [options]
Subcommands.Description: Browse the curated workflow example library

Subcommands.Name: run [options]
Subcommands.Description: Run a workflow or coded file via Studio (no debugging). Use `debug start` to launch with breakpoints.

Subcommands.Name: debug [options]
Subcommands.Description: Drive an active debug session — start, step, break, continue

Subcommands.Name: execution [options]
Subcommands.Description: Lifecycle of the active execution — cancel (and future status/list)

Subcommands.Name: help [command]
Subcommands.Description: display help for command

GlobalOptions.Description: JMESPath expression applied to the Data field of the response envelope
Write logs to file instead of stderr
